scope: Public carrier network inference only. Not a live schedule, rate, capacity, or booking dataset.

sources.maersk_ocean_transport: Maersk Global Ocean Transport Services, public carrier page; states ocean freight to 500+ global destinations and provides cargo solutions.
sources.maersk_schedules: Maersk public schedules page; route/vessel/port-call schedule lookup concept.
sources.msc_trade_services: MSC Trade Services official page; states 300 global routes, 520 ports, 155+ countries, 675 offices.
sources.cma_cgm_line_services: CMA CGM Shipping Lines Search; line services by trade/zones and schedules.
sources.cma_cgm_schedules: CMA CGM schedules search; point-to-point schedule lookup up to listed period.
sources.hapag_lloyd_schedule: Hapag-Lloyd Vessel Schedule; detailed route and real-time schedule data concept.
sources.hapag_lloyd_services: Hapag-Lloyd services information; reefer, special cargo and dangerous goods service categories.
sources.cosco_schedule: COSCO Shipping Lines official schedule pages; service categories such as Trans-Pacific, Europe, Europe Feeder, Trans-Atlantic, Intra-Asia, Oceania, Asia/South Africa/South America.
sources.one_service_maps: ONE official service maps; global routes/lanes, sailing/vessel schedules, terminals, port calls and port-to-port details.
sources.evergreen_portal: Evergreen Shipping Agency portal; sailing schedules, booking and cargo tracking portal; registration required for some services.

known_limitations: SEA-D does not include live schedules, space, capacity or rates.
Exact port-pair service requires live carrier schedule lookup.
DG, reefer, OOG and project cargo require carrier-specific acceptance/approval.
Carrier public sites can change; schedule data is time-sensitive.
Broad trade-lane families are inference/reference, not contractual service commitments.
